---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-03"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# ======================================================
# IHC Concordance Analysis (Kappa)
# ======================================================

# 1. Install/Load Library
if(!require(irr)) install.packages("irr")
if(!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
library(irr)
library(dplyr)

# 2. Load Data
# Ensure "IHC score_for kappa.csv" is in your R working directory
data <- read.csv("/Users/pichaya/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Personal/Postdoc_HNSCC/Xenium/IHC score_for kappa.csv")

# 3. Rename columns to avoid errors with spaces/parentheses
colnames(data)[4] <- "Human_Score"
colnames(data)[5] <- "QuPath_Score"

# 4. Global Analysis (All Markers)
print("--- GLOBAL RESULTS ---")
kappa_global <- kappa2(data[, c("Human_Score", "QuPath_Score")], weight = "squared")
print(paste("Global Weighted Kappa:", round(kappa_global$value, 3)))

# 5. Analysis Per Marker
print("--- PER MARKER RESULTS ---")
marker_results <- data %>%
  group_by(Marker) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    # Quadratic Weighted Kappa
    Kappa = kappa2(cbind(Human_Score, QuPath_Score), weight = "squared")$value,
    # Spearman Correlation (Trend)
    Correlation = cor(Human_Score, QuPath_Score, method = "spearman"),
    # Bias (Positive = QuPath is higher; Negative = QuPath is lower)
    Bias = mean(QuPath_Score - Human_Score)
  )

# Print as a clean table
print(as.data.frame(marker_results))

# 6. Save results to file (optional)
write.csv(marker_results, "/Users/pichaya/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-Personal/Postdoc_HNSCC/Xenium/IHC_Kappa_Results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
